text:
*[Bug 19017] New: Typo/bug in ar.yw.default,  ar.ols, ar.burg.default*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19017>

            Bug ID: 19017
           Summary: Typo/bug in ar.yw.default,  ar.ols,  ar.burg.default
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Analyses
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:sgiannerini@gmail.com|sgiannerini@gmail.com>

I think there is a (small) bug in  

  ar.yw.default   (line 142 of the source file ar.R)
  ar.ols          (line 429 of the source file ar.R)
  ar.burg.default (line 570 of the source file ar.R)

```
 maic <- min(aic)
```
should be 
```
 maic <- min(xaic)
```

Note that, apparently, this typo/bug does little harm since the subsequent line
of code
```
   xaic <- setNames(if(is.finite(maic)) xaic - min(xaic) else
                     ifelse(xaic == maic, 0, Inf), 0L:order.max)
```
does not use `maic` but `min(xaic)` directly. In any case, after fixing, one
could replace `min(xaic)` with `maic` to avoid recomputing `min(xaic)`. 

Kind regards

Simone

  Tested against R-devel 2026-03-06

Note that, apparently, this typo/bug does little harm since the subsequent line

xaic <- setNames(if(is.finite(maic)) xaic - min(xaic) else
                     ifelse(xaic == maic, 0, Inf), 0L:order.max)

directly. In any case, after fixing, one
could replace

. 

Kind regards

Simone

  Tested against R-devel 2026-03-06

text:
*[Bug 19018] New: The qpdf binary now seems to require filenames ending in .pdf*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19018>

            Bug ID: 19018
           Summary: The qpdf binary now seems to require filenames ending
                    in .pdf
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Misc
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:edd@debian.org|edd@debian.org>

Created attachment 3622
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3622&action=edit>
Suggested one-line diff to render compactPDF() resilient to qpdf sillyness

Pdf vignettes were no longer compressing on my system which seemed odd, and I
eventually got to the bottom where I can show that this seems to stem from a
change in `qpdf`, and one we can overcome.

First off, the error on R-release (using `verbose=3`)

```
> tools::compactPDF("vignette.pdf", gs_quality="ebook", verbose=3)
qs_quality="ebook" : use_gs=TRUE, use_qpdf=TRUE
#{pdf}s = length(paths) = 1
- vignette.pdf:
   gs: res=<>;  + qpdf: res=qpdf: open /tmp/r/Rtmp1AKJGN/pdf2725da5bc1b9f3:
Permission denied; 
Warning message:
In system2(qpdf, c(qpdf_flags, shQuote(tf2), shQuote(tf)), verb2,  :
  running command ''/usr/bin/qpdf' --object-streams=generate
'/tmp/r/Rtmp1AKJGN/pdf2725da5bc1b9f3' '/tmp/r/Rtmp1AKJGN/pdf2725da78dfc024'
2>&1' had status 2
> 
```

We see that this errors on 'permission denied'. Which makes no sense: it is our
own temporary directory for the R session. So if this is not it, it may be
`qpdf`.

I think I can 'demonstrate' that via a 2x2 setup of {input,output} \time
{with,without} pdf extension.  A simple script is

```
#!/usr/bin/env RDscript

R.version

inpdf <- "vignette.pdf"

tfo1 <- tempfile("pdf")
tfo2 <- tempfile("pdf", fileext=".pdf")
tfi1 <- tempfile("pdf")
tfi2 <- tempfile("pdf", fileext=".pdf")

res <- file.copy(inpdf, tfi1)
res <- file.copy(inpdf, tfi2)

cat("If either in or out does not have .pdf, we error\n")
r1 <- system2("qpdf", c("--object-streams=generate", tfi1, tfo1), stderr=NULL)
r2 <- system2("qpdf", c("--object-streams=generate", tfi1, tfo2), stderr=NULL)
r3 <- system2("qpdf", c("--object-streams=generate", tfi2, tfo1), stderr=NULL)

cat("Only good one is .pdf to .pdf\n")
r4 <- system2("qpdf", c("--object-streams=generate", tfi2, tfo2))

print(c(r1,r2,r3,r4))

## ## R-devel 'as is'
## $ RDscript qpdf_extension_bugreport.R
##                _
## platform       x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
## arch           x86_64
## os             linux-gnu
## system         x86_64, linux-gnu
## status         Under development (unstable)
## major          4
## minor          6.0
## year           2026
## month          03
## day            10
## svn rev        89585
## language       R
## version.string R Under development (unstable) (2026-03-10 r89585)
## nickname       Unsuffered Consequences
## If either in or out does not have .pdf, we error
## Only good one is .pdf to .pdf
## [1] 2 2 2 0
## $
```

The bottom contains the output from today's R-devel. Only in the 'input and
output file have a .pdf extension' case do we get to a return code of 0 as
desired. All three other cases error.

Fixing this is a one-liner (also attached):

```
edd<@U043URAARCP>:~/svn/r-devel$ svn diff
Index: src/library/tools/R/admin.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/tools/R/admin.R (revision 89590)
+++ src/library/tools/R/admin.R (working copy)
@@ -1129,7 +1129,8 @@
     dummy <- <http://rep.int|rep.int>(NA_real_, length(paths))
     ans <- data.frame(old = dummy, new = dummy, row.names = paths)
     ## These should not have spaces, but quote below to be safe.
-    tf <- tempfile("pdf"); tf2 <- tempfile("pdf")
+    ## Some (newer ?) 'qpdf' versions want the '.pdf' file extension

+++ src/library/tools/R/admin.R (working copy)
@@ -1129,7 +1129,8 @@
     dummy <-

(NA_real_, length(paths))
     ans <- data.frame(old = dummy, new = dummy, row.names = paths)
     ## These should not have spaces, but quote below to be safe.
-    tf <- tempfile("pdf"); tf2 <- tempfile("pdf")

text:
+    tf <- tempfile("pdf", fileext=".pdf"); tf2 <- tempfile("pdf",
fileext=".pdf")
     verb2 <- verbose >= 2
     for (p in paths) {
         res <- 0
edd<@U043URAARCP>:~/svn/r-devel$
```

And with that fix, the example above which errored in R-release now works work
in the (patched) R-devel:

```
> tools::compactPDF("vignette.pdf", gs_quality="ebook", verbose=3)
qs_quality="ebook" : use_gs=TRUE, use_qpdf=TRUE
#{pdf}s = length(paths) = 1
- vignette.pdf:
   gs: res=<>;  + qpdf: res=<>; 
    ==> (new=181381)/(old=520014) = 0.3488 =====> using it !!
  compacted ‘vignette.pdf’ from 508Kb to 177Kb
> 
```

My `qpdf` is

```
$ qpdf --version
qpdf version 12.2.0
Run qpdf --copyright to see copyright and license information.
$ 
```

text:
*[Bug 19018] The qpdf binary now seems to require filenames ending in .pdf*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19018>

--- Comment #1 from Dirk Eddelbuettel (<mailto:edd@debian.org|edd@debian.org>) ---
For comparison here is Debian testing with a marginally newer `qpdf`.  Maybe
the issue I encountered is specific to the one I see in Ubuntu 25.10

```
> tools::compactPDF("vignette.pdf", gs_quality="ebook", verbose=3)
qs_quality="ebook" : use_gs=TRUE, use_qpdf=TRUE
#{pdf}s = length(paths) = 1
- vignette.pdf:
   gs: res=<>;  + qpdf: res=<>; 
    ==> (new=181365)/(old=520014) = 0.348769 =====> using it !!
  compacted ‘vignette.pdf’ from 508Kb to 177Kb
> system("qpdf --version")
qpdf version 12.3.2
Run qpdf --copyright to see copyright and license information.
> 
```
